## [24:22 - 27:23]

If you learn this exercise, it will be useful to you in any situation where you're communicating with somebody else to help them better themselves. And that's the feature benefit outcome translation. So when you look at something, you say, is this a feature or a benefit or an outcome? And if it's a feature, turn it into a benefit. And if it's a benefit, turn it into an outcome. So we always do this translation. So the feature would be the drill. I got a drill, right? The benefit is this drill makes it easy to make a hole. Now there's a hole. The outcome is now I can hang that beautiful picture of my family on the wall that I can look at and feel loved and show my love. So the drill is what it is. The benefit is what it does. And the outcome is what can occur based on what it has done. So another example here is the webinar is just a feature. For making money, a webinar is just a feature. It's a tool in toolbox. The benefit is the webinar often can make more money than many other things that you could use in place of a webinar or in addition or besides a webinar. But the outcome, so think about it, if a webinar is the feature and the benefit is it makes you a ton of money, the outcome is you never have to look at a price tag again when you go shopping. And so that is the specific way in which a benefit can show up in somebody's life. So a feature is what it is. A benefit is what it does. And an outcome is how it changes your life. So everywhere in your webinar presentation, you should do an audit of how many features am I using, are enough of them being translated into benefits, and are enough of those benefits being translated to outcome.
